$schema_note: Machine-readable training/eval set for distributed BI reasoning. Fictional retail-at-scale scenarios only. Score an agent on reasoning match + correct ending artifact. Mirrors references/agent-training-set.md.

items.question: One month of one region from a date-partitioned billion-row table. Use Spark?
items.expected_reasoning: No; post-pruning it's a few GB. Single-node (DuckDB/Polars) or warehouse query. Single-node first.

items.question: Joining billion-row orders to the small stores dim - best strategy?
items.expected_reasoning: Broadcast the small dimension so orders is never shuffled.

items.question: Groupby on store_id is 99% done then one task hangs for an hour. Why and fix?
items.expected_reasoning: Flagship-store skew; hot key concentrates on one task. Fix: AQE skew-join, then broadcast other side, then salt the hot key.

items.question: Revenue too high and shuffle huge after joining orders to returns.
items.expected_reasoning: One-to-many fan-out double-counts and inflates shuffle; pre-aggregate returns to order-line grain, then join many-to-one.

items.question: Dashboard needs distinct customers per region; exact count is slow. Approximate OK?
items.expected_reasoning: Yes for a tolerant dashboard; use approx-distinct and label approximate; exact for financial reconciliation.

items.question: Can I .collect() the enriched billion-row frame to check it?
items.expected_reasoning: No; collect funnels everything to the driver and OOMs it. Inspect with count / limit+sample / aggregates.

items.question: A backfill rerun doubled last week's totals. Why?
items.expected_reasoning: Append-only writes aren't idempotent; use partition-overwrite or merge-on-key; verify with a double-run.

items.question: Yesterday's revenue is understated because mobile orders sync late. Fix?
items.expected_reasoning: Add an idempotent late-data lookback window that reprocesses recent partitions.

items.question: The job finished successfully - is the result reconciled?
items.expected_reasoning: No; success != correctness. Compute distributed control totals, match to source/SQL, hand record to the gate.

items.question: Should I coalesce(1) to get a single output file?
items.expected_reasoning: No; it funnels all data through one core, killing parallelism and risking OOM. Right-size partitions or use table-format compaction.

items.question: Clickstream partitioned by minute is slow to query. Why and fix?
items.expected_reasoning: Small-files problem from over-partitioning; partition by date/hour and compact.

items.question: A selective date filter still scans the whole table. Why?
items.expected_reasoning: Filter not pushed down / layout not date-partitioned; make the filter pushable and verify pruning in the plan.

items.question: Is it fine to sum each region's average basket value across regions at scale?
items.expected_reasoning: No; averages are non-additive (PY-CN-054). Recompute weighted at target grain; summing also defeats partial aggregation.

items.question: Duplicate event_ids appear after dedup. What went wrong?
items.expected_reasoning: Dedup was per-partition; duplicates span partitions, so dedup must be global on the grain key.

items.question: Engine choice: a heavy join over data already in the warehouse?
items.expected_reasoning: Push the join down to the warehouse (SQL layer) rather than extracting into distributed Python; keeps data in place and respects the boundary.
